subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Quantitatively analyze energy flow using 10% rule and pyramid calculations
Model and calculate biomagnification effects with numerical examples
Critically evaluate ozone depletion data, catalytic mechanisms, and recovery trends
Analyze waste decomposition rates and propose quantitative reduction strategies
Assess human impact through case studies and predict ecological consequences
Develop advanced skills: data analysis, evidence-based arguments, sustainable solution design

lesson_plan.title: Ecosystem Dynamics – Quantitative Energy Flow
topics_covered: Pyramids of energy, numbers, biomass – interpretation
student_activities: Calculate energy at each trophic level (e.g., 10000 kJ producers → herbivores → carnivores)
Draw and label energy pyramid with values
Analyze why vegetarian diet is more efficient

lesson_plan.title: Biomagnification – Modeling and Analysis
topics_covered: Real examples: DDT, mercury in food chain
teaching_strategy: Critical discussion on human health risks
student_activities: Model biomagnification: assume 1 unit toxin at producer → calculate at top level (10% retention)

lesson_plan.title: Ozone Depletion – Advanced Chemistry and Data
topics_covered: UV effects: DNA damage, ecosystem impact
student_activities: Write and explain Cl catalytic cycle equations

lesson_plan.title: Waste Management – Quantitative and Sustainable Analysis
topics_covered: Quantitative waste audit and reduction modeling
student_activities: Conduct mini waste audit; calculate % biodegradable

lesson_plan.title: Human Impact – Case Studies and Prediction
student_activities: Analyze one case: causes, effects, solutions

lesson_plan.title: Synthesis, Advanced Assessment, and Innovation
student_activities: Solve advanced problems (energy calc, biomagnification, ozone data)
Design one innovative eco-solution with justification
Critically evaluate a claim (e.g., 'recycling solves plastic crisis')

teaching_aids: Charts: energy pyramids, biomagnification, ozone cycle
Graph paper, calculators for quantitative work
Waste samples, projector for case studies
teacher_tip: Incorporate real data (e.g., ozone trends from NASA) and calculators for authenticity. Push for evidence-based arguments and quantitative justification. Connect to current events (plastic treaty, ozone recovery) to motivate deeper thinking.
